evals.prompt: I have a research paper at /Users/ryanxu/CodeBase/GPTase/papers/rothlisberger_2008_kemp_elimination_catalysts.pdf that I want to extract to Markdown. My MinerU token is already stored in the project's .env file as MINERU_TOKEN. Please extract it and save the result to /tmp/rothlisberger_out/. I care about getting formula and table content right.
evals.expected_output: A Python script or step-by-step instructions using the MinerU cloud API (3-step flow: get pre-signed URL, upload without Content-Type header, poll batch_id), loading MINERU_TOKEN from .env via dotenv. Should NOT use local mineru CLI or flash-extract.

assertions.name: uses_cloud_api_endpoint
assertions.description: Output references the MinerU cloud API endpoint (mineru.net/api/v4/file-urls/batch)

assertions.name: loads_token_from_env
assertions.description: Output loads MINERU_TOKEN from .env (e.g., load_dotenv or dotenv)

assertions.name: no_content_type_on_upload
assertions.description: Output either omits Content-Type on the PUT/upload step or explicitly warns not to set it

assertions.name: polls_batch_id
assertions.description: Output polls using batch_id (not a separate submit endpoint)

assertions.name: does_not_use_local_cli
assertions.description: Output does NOT recommend running the local 'mineru' CLI command for this task

evals.prompt: I've got about 15 PDFs sitting in /Users/ryanxu/CodeBase/GPTase/papers/ (all the *.pdf files). I want to extract all of them to Markdown using MinerU. My token is in .env as MINERU_TOKEN. Each PDF should get its own output directory named after the PDF stem (e.g., papers/rothlisberger_2008_kemp_elimination_catalysts/). What's the most efficient way to do this?
evals.expected_output: A batch approach that uploads all PDFs in a SINGLE API request (not one-by-one), uses ThreadPoolExecutor for concurrent uploads, and polls a single batch_id for all results. Should NOT loop and call the API once per file.

assertions.name: single_batch_request
assertions.description: Output sends all files in one POST to file-urls/batch (not one request per file)

assertions.name: concurrent_upload
assertions.description: Output uses concurrent upload (ThreadPoolExecutor or asyncio), not sequential upload

assertions.name: single_poll_loop
assertions.description: Output polls a single batch_id for all results, not one poll loop per file

assertions.name: per_stem_output_dirs
assertions.description: Output saves results to directories named after each PDF stem

evals.prompt: Hey, can you quickly pull out the text from this PDF: /tmp/meeting_notes.pdf? I just need a rough markdown version. I don't have any MinerU API token or account set up.
evals.expected_output: Should fall back to flash-extract CLI (mineru-open-api flash-extract) since no token is available and the PDF is described as simple. Should NOT try to use the cloud API or local pipeline.

assertions.name: uses_flash_extract
assertions.description: Output recommends or uses 'mineru-open-api flash-extract' command

assertions.name: does_not_require_token
assertions.description: Output does not require or prompt for a MINERU_TOKEN

assertions.name: does_not_use_cloud_api
assertions.description: Output does not use the cloud API Python flow (no file-urls/batch endpoint)
